name: DCS Procurement Manual — PRN, Contract Register & Risk Assessment (low+ value, non-ICT/PS)
description: DCS obligations for goods, services, construction and labour hire procurements at low value ($10k–$50k) and above. Covers the Procurement Reference Number (PRN), DCS Central Contracts Register upload, and Combined Risk Assessment template. Note: the exact PRN threshold is $30,000 — this scheme fires at the 'low' tier ($10k+) as a prompt to check the $30k threshold.

pre_checks.title: Check whether $30k (incl. GST) threshold is met for PRN and Contract Register
pre_checks.body: The DCS PRN and Central Contracts Register thresholds are both $30,000 inclusive of GST. If your total engagement value equals or exceeds $30,000 incl. GST, the PRN and Contract Register obligations below apply. This value tier fires at $10k+ as a prompt — confirm your actual value against the $30k threshold.

obligations.title: Procurement Reference Number (PRN) required (≥$30k incl. GST)
obligations.body: Request a Procurement Reference Number (PRN) from DCS Procurement before any market activity for all goods, services, construction, and labour hire procurements with a total value of $30,000 or more (incl. GST). All procurements above $30k must also be registered in the DCS Central Contracts Register within 21 days of contract execution. Contact the DCS Procurement helpdesk to obtain a PRN.

obligations.title: DCS Combined Risk Assessment template required (≥$30k)
obligations.body: Use the DCS Combined Risk Assessment template (Business Risk tab) for all goods and services procurements with a value of $30,000 or more. The risk assessment must be completed and approved before proceeding to market. Risk assessments below $250k can be approved at Grade 11/12 or above. For procurements involving customer or employee data, or requiring integration with DCS IT infrastructure, obtain Procurement Risk Assessment approval from the Chief Information Officer before proceeding.

obligations.title: DCS Central Contracts Register upload within 21 days of execution
obligations.body: Upload contract details to the DCS Central Contracts Register within 21 days of contract execution. This applies to: all contracts $30,000 or more (incl. GST), all contracts with Aboriginal suppliers (any value), all consultancy engagements (any value), operating leases above $10,000, contracts with heightened modern slavery risk (any value), and standing offer contracts likely to exceed $150,000.
